title: Cognitive Firewalls Foundry

fields.vision: The Cognitive Firewalls Foundry is the MuranoOS room that holds Nik Murano's capstone (Georgetown Applied Intelligence, cognitive-firewalls thesis) as a controlled experimental and interpretive system rather than a prose note page.
fields.mechanism: , state the backend/frontend or packet components, and decide whether the handoff is ready for engineering, public explanation, or archival retention
fields.source_utilization: Discovery, creativity, systems thinking, and project generation. Use source library creativity/innovation/network sources to professionalize the project-development account. Vision: Explain Nexus and Personal OS as project-development and discovery infrastructure, not miscellaneous personal tooling. Mechanism: Extract creativity, network, learning, imagination, leadership, and project-generation mechanisms. Plan: Rewrite Nexus and planning pages around upstream/downstream project movement. Status: source library identified as candidate scholarly support; direct quotation requires exact passage review. Remaining gap: Need exact project-method and creativity passages. Source pool: 151 deduplicated local title candidates; 154 catalogue support rows. Examples: Creativity Research; The Heritage, Creativity and Innovation Nexus. source library scholarly-source routing with author correction. Raw filenames often append Nik_Murano and are not treated as authorship evidence. Catalogue rows are preferred for authors where available. This strengthens page vision, mechanism, source-use, plan, and gap language, but it does not certify passage-level citation support. source-library controlled content is source evidence for internal scholarship and planning; do not redistribute source files or long excerpts in the public package.
fields.plan: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. When to reach for this: You need to (a) support Nik's active capstone thesis work inside MuranoOS under Screen 13 / Academic Workstation; (b) design a factorial experimental harness that preserves evidence contamination state and AI-assisted run comparability; (c) generate decision-quality export packets for advisor/committee review. Entry point: README.md — 01_Product_Brief.md — 02_Room_Architecture.md — 04_UI_UX_Spec.md — 08_Data_Model.md — 10_Implementation_Plan.md — 11_QA_and_Acceptance.md. Integration target: MuranoOS desktop app, Screen 13 (Academic Workstation). Data model aligns with the three mandatory capstone_* tables already anticipated in the capstone direction. Wire or preserve the implementation files: None. Spec-only v1 package. The README explicitly acknowledges: "this does not block implementation" but nothing here is scaffolded yet — the handoff is synthesized from surviving workbooks and extracted architectural constraints. Two Excel reference workbooks (Red_Cell_Transposition_Toolkit.xlsx v1 and v2) supply substantive analytic content for pattern-card seeding.
fields.remaining_gaps: Promote the packet from documented handoff to live implementation only after the declared caveats are resolved: apply or wire the scaffold, run the stated validation or smoke-test sequence, and keep unresolved assumptions visible.
fields.detailed_source_vision: The Cognitive Firewalls Foundry is the MuranoOS room that holds Nik Murano's capstone (Georgetown Applied Intelligence, cognitive-firewalls thesis) as a controlled experimental and interpretive system rather than a prose note page. It is an instrument for moving from scenario — run — evidence review — failure-mode tagging — pattern card — countermeasure proposal — export packet without context-switching and without losing provenance. The room is recommended as a deep sub-room under Screen 13 / Academic Workstation (Academic Workstation > Cognitive Firewalls Foundry). Its explicit architectural stance is "instrument, not a dashboard." Success is defined by preservation of: scenarios, evidence packets, structured-analytic procedures (SATs), run logs, milestone pressure, and cross-project conceptual resonance — none of which a flat notes app can preserve.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: , state the backend/frontend or packet components, and decide whether the handoff is ready for engineering, public explanation, or archival retention

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: The Cognitive Firewalls Foundry is the MuranoOS room that holds Nik Murano's capstone (Georgetown Applied Intelligence, cognitive-firewalls thesis) as a controlled experimental and interpretive system rather than a prose note page.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: , state the backend/frontend or packet components, and decide whether the handoff is ready for engineering, public explanation, or archival retention
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It translates the capstone idea into operational checks a reviewer can actually use against AI-mediated evidence corruption and analytic drift.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: blueprint detailed source fields.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.

instruments.proof: Its explicit architectural stance is "instrument, not a dashboard." Success is defined by preservation of: scenarios, evidence packets, structured-analytic procedures (SATs), run logs, milestone pressure, and cross-project conceptual resonance — none of which.
instruments.gap: Promote the packet from documented handoff to live implementation only after the declared caveats are resolved: apply or wire the scaffold, run the stated validation or smoke-test sequence, and keep unresolved.
instruments.verification_check: Confirm `Instrument, Not A Dashboard` appears in the cited source context and that the public page explains what output, decision, or project function it supports.
